_meta.description: Rules for assembling a dungeon map from the content files. This file is the engine spec — it defines how rooms, enemies, NPCs, traps, events, and loot are selected, placed, and connected during procedural generation.

map_parameters.description: Top-level parameters for a dungeon map generation run.

tier_distribution.description: What percentage of non-unique rooms should come from each tier bracket.
tier_distribution.note: Unique rooms (entrance_gate, throne_room, treasure_vault) are always placed regardless of distribution.

depth_axis.description: The map has a conceptual depth. Tier 1 rooms go near the start (depth 0–2), tier 5 near the end (depth 8+). This is a soft constraint — deviation creates interesting layouts but shouldn't be ignored entirely.

required_rooms.description: Rooms that must appear exactly once on every map.

rooms.placement: first room — the map's starting point
rooms.connections: must connect to exactly one other room
must connect to at least 2 rooms including treasure_vault
rooms.requires_adjacent: at least one of: crypt, armory, ritual_circle

rooms.placement: always adjacent to throne_room, always at max_depth

optional_unique_rooms.description: Rooms that appear at most once per map, selected by weight.
optional_unique_rooms.selection: weighted random from list — all are tier 3+ so place in mid/deep dungeon
optional_unique_rooms.placement_rule: secret_room must not be directly adjacent to entrance_gate, throne_room, or treasure_vault

room_connections.description: Rules for connecting rooms into a graph.
room_connections.method: Start from entrance_gate. Extend a main path to throne_room (3–6 rooms). Branch off the main path to fill remaining room count. No dead ends permitted except treasure_vault and secret_room.
room_connections.branching_factor: Each room on the main path has a 40% chance of generating one side branch (1–2 rooms). Side branches may branch once more at 20% probability.
room_connections.loops: Generate at least one loop — a secondary path that rejoins the main path, creating an alternate route between two non-adjacent rooms.
room_connections.connection_flavor: When two rooms are connected, the corridor between them is described by the lower-tier room's corridor flavor or a generic torch_hall description if the room has no corridor text.

locked_doors.key_must_precede_door: The room containing the key must be reachable without passing through the locked door.

enemy_placement.description: Rules for placing enemies in rooms.
enemy_placement.selection_rule: Choose from enemy pool where enemy.tier <= room.tier + 1 and enemy.tags overlap with room.tags where possible.

boss_rules.throne_room: Always contains undead_king. No other enemies on spawn (they appear in phase 2).
boss_rules.rooms_with_tag_boss_arena: Always contain exactly one boss or boss_minor enemy.

exclusions: secret_room — no enemies (by default; override allowed for trap-style secret rooms)
enemy_placement.npc_and_enemy_coexist: NPCs with archetype != 'rival' should not share a room with enemies. If both are assigned to the same room, remove the enemy and place it in an adjacent room instead.

npc_placement.selection_rule: Choose NPCs where npc.tier <= room.tier + 1. Check npc.tags against room.tags for compatibility.
npc_placement.npc_oracle_rule: npc_dungeon_oracle appears at most once per map, in the secret_room if present, otherwise in a tier 3+ room.

trap_placement.selection_rule: Choose trap where trap.tier <= room.tier + 1 and trap.tags include at least one room.tag.
exclusions: treasure_vault — no traps (the vault has already been earned)

event_placement.assignment: Environmental events are assigned to rooms during play (they trigger on visits). Interactive events are anchored to specific room types.
event_placement.environmental_event_distribution: Distribute environmental events across all non-unique rooms. No room gets more than 2 environmental events.
event_placement.event_secret_door_hint_rule: If a secret_room is on the map, event_secret_door_hint must be placed in an adjacent room.

floor_loot.table_selection: Use loot_floor_tier{room.tier}. If no tier-specific table exists, use the nearest lower tier.

chests.chest_frequency: One chest per 4–5 rooms on average.

special_loot.treasure_vault: Use loot_chest_legendary table regardless of other rules.

quest_assignment.quest_npc_placement_rule: If a quest is selected for a map run, its giver NPC must be placed. Not all quests need to be available every run — select 1–2 side quests per map.

difficulty_modifiers.description: Optional modifiers applied to the whole map at generation time.

hard.note: All DCs increased by 2. Enemy counts at the high end of ranges.

nightmare.special: The Undead King enters Phase 2 at 50 HP instead of 33 HP. The Cursed Crown reduces max HP by 10 instead of 5.

validation_checklist.description: Run this checklist on a generated map before presenting it.
checks: entrance_gate exists and is at depth 0
throne_room exists and is at max depth - 1
treasure_vault exists adjacent to throne_room at max depth
treasure_vault is reachable without passing through a locked door that blocks the warden_key or signet_ring
No room at depth 0 or 1 contains a tier 4 or 5 enemy
No enemy is in the same room as a non-rival NPC
At least one healing item is accessible before depth 3
If secret_room exists, event_secret_door_hint is in an adjacent room
The warden_key is obtainable (from crypt_warden drop or present in a chest) before the vault is reached
At least one torch or light source is obtainable in the first 3 rooms
